title: dactylorhiza umbrosa subsp. magna
intro: Dactylorhiza umbrosa subsp. magna is a subspecies of orchid, commonly known as the alpine marsh orchid, which is native to the mountainous regions of Central and Eastern Europe, as well as parts of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat various health issues such as digestive problems, inflammation, and respiratory issues due to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits associated with Dactylorhiza umbrosa subsp. magna include its potential to reduce stress, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions, although it is essential to consult with a medical professional before using any herbal remedy.
